chunk_summary: This section outlines the WHO ten-step care model for managing severe acute malnutrition (SAM), focusing on inpatient stabilization and outpatient rehabilitation, with specific steps to prevent early deaths and ensure recovery.
learning_objectives: Understand the ten-step WHO care model for SAM management.
Differentiate between inpatient and outpatient care for complicated and uncomplicated SAM cases.
Learn the timeline and focus areas for stabilization, transition, and rehabilitation phases.
key_facts: The ten-step model addresses early deaths in SAM, often caused by iatrogenic errors.
Steps 1–7 focus on stabilization (days 0–2), including hypoglycemia, hypothermia, and dehydration management.
Electrolyte correction involves potassium and magnesium, with sodium restriction.
Cautious feeding starts with F-75 (small, frequent feeds) and transitions to F-100 or RUTF for catch-up growth.
Week 2 onward emphasizes developmental support and structured outpatient follow-up.
reference_quote: Early deaths in SAM are often iatrogenic (fluid and feeding errors) and danger signs may be muted.
